description_long: This object is a compact, double-barreled shotgun with a striking, fiery red and orange pattern covering its entire exterior. The barrels are arranged side-by-side, both short in length, giving the weapon a sawed-off appearance. The grip is curved and lacks a traditional shoulder stock, making it more maneuverable but harder to control. The trigger guard is rounded and proportionally sized to the grip, with no visible reinforcement or finger grooves. The surface finish is matte with a slightly rough texture, and the overall build suggests a metal core with a decorative, possibly synthetic, outer layer. The shotgun is designed for close-quarters use and would be found in urban environments where concealability and ease of handling are prioritized.
description: A compact, side-by-side double-barrel shotgun with a fiery red-orange pattern and a curved grip, lacking a full stock. Its short barrels and matte finish give it a distinctive, urban-ready appearance.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the shotgun, highlighting the side-by-side barrels, curved grip, and vibrant fiery pattern.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the twin barrel openings and the symmetrical, patterned muzzle.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring the first but with the trigger and guard more visible against the patterned body.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the grip and the back of the barrels, with the pattern continuing along the length.
attribute_barrel_configuration: side-by-side double-barrel, both barrels short and of equal length, no visible ribbing or venting
attribute_trigger_guard_design: rounded, medium-sized trigger guard with no visible reinforcement or finger grooves
